examples: Fruit an aggregate, spicate, 10-12 cm long; follicles long ellipsoid, obovoid or ovoid, apex obtuse or abruptly mucronate.
Inflorescences thyrses, either subpaniculate below and spicate-racemose above or with numerous, mostly 2-flowered branchlets, pedicels 1 – 2 (-16) mm.
Brazilian shrub having twice-pinnate leaves and small spicate flowers followed by flat or irregularly torulose pods; sometimes placed in genus Piptadenia.
